G2744.derivation: from some (obsolete) base akin to that of (to boast) and G2172 (εὔχομαι);

G4656.strongs_def: to obscure or blind (literally or figuratively)

G4149.strongs_def: wealth (as fulness), i.e. (literally) money, possessions, or (figuratively) abundance, richness, (specially), valuable bestowment

G2431.strongs_def: propitious or merry ("hilarious"), i.e. prompt or willing

G5146.strongs_def: properly, a crow-foot (three-pronged obstruction in war), i.e. (by analogy) a thorny plant (caltrop)

G3569.strongs_def: the things now, i.e. (adverbially) at present

G255.derivation: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and the base of G3081 (λυσιτελεῖ);

G3475.strongs_def: Moseus, Moses, or Mouses (i.e. Mosheh), the Hebrew lawgiver

G4439.strongs_def: a gate, i.e. the leaf or wing of a folding entrance (literally or figuratively)

G873.strongs_def: to set off by boundary, i.e. (figuratively) limit, exclude, appoint, etc.

G732.derivation: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of G4517 (ῥώννυμι);

G4445.strongs_def: to be on fire, i.e. (specially), to have a fever

G226.strongs_def: to be true (in doctrine and profession)

G1098.strongs_def: sweet wine, i.e. (properly) must (fresh juice), but used of the more saccharine (and therefore highly inebriating) fermented wine

G537.derivation: from G1 (Α) (as a particle of union) and G3956 (πᾶς);

G4599.strongs_def: probably akin to the base of G2476 (ἵστημι)); to strengthen, i.e. (figuratively) confirm (in spiritual knowledge and power)

G1705.strongs_def: to fill in (up), i.e. (by implication) to satisfy (literally or figuratively)
G1705.derivation: from G1722 (ἐν) and the base of G4118 (πλεῖστος);

G1680.derivation: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

G176.derivation: from G1 (Α) (as a negative particle) and a derivative of G2607 (καταγινώσκω);

G2340.strongs_def: to hunt (an animal), i.e. (figuratively) to carp at

G2098.strongs_def: a good message, i.e. the gospel

G4486.derivation: both prolonged forms of (which appears only in certain forms, and is itself probably a strengthened form of (see in G2608 (κατάγνυμι))) to "break," "wreck" or "crack", i.e. (especially) to sunder (by separation of the parts;
G4486.strongs_def: G2608 (κατάγνυμι) being its intensive (with the preposition in composition), and G2352 (θραύω) a shattering to minute fragments; but not a reduction to the constituent particles, like G3089 (λύω)) or disrupt, lacerate; by implication, to convulse (with spasms); figuratively, to give vent to joyful emotions

G3870.strongs_def: to call near, i.e. invite, invoke (by imploration, hortation or consolation)

H1.strongs_def: father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application

H6.strongs_def: properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)

H7.strongs_def: {properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy)}

H16.derivation: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of bending toward);

H19.derivation: from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn);

H22.derivation: from H1 (אָב) and H410 (אֵל); father (i.e. possessor) of God;

H108.derivation: from the same as H181 (אוּד) (in the sense of enveloping);

H115.derivation: dual from H142 (אָדַר) (in the sense of eminence); double mound;

H119.derivation: to show blood (in the face), i.e.

H135.derivation: intensive from the same as H134 (אֶדֶן); firm;

H137.derivation: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H966 (בֶּזֶק); lord of Bezek;

H138.derivation: original (prolonged) אֲדֹנִיָּהוּ ; from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H3050 (יָהּ); lord (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;

H154.derivation: from the equivalent of H153 (אֶדְרָע); mighty;

H159.strongs_def: {affection (in a good or a bad sense)}

H160.derivation: feminine of H158 (אַהַב) and meaning the same
H160.strongs_def: {affection (in a good or a bad sense)}

H161.derivation: from an unused root meaning to be united; unity;

H180.derivation: or (shortened) אֻבָל ; from H2986 (יָבַל) (in the sense of H2988 (יָבָל));

H181.derivation: from an unused root meaning to rake together;

H182.derivation: or (shortened) אֹדוֹת (only thus in the plural); from the same as H181 (אוּד);

H183.kjv_def: covet, (greatly) desire, be desirous, long, lust (after).

H186.derivation: perhaps by permutation for H5813 (עֻזִּי), strong;

H188.derivation: probably from H183 (אָוָה) (in the sense of crying out after);

H191.derivation: from an unused root (meaning to be perverse);

H193.derivation: from an unused root meaning to twist, i.e. (by implication) be strong;

H197.derivation: or (shortened), אֻלָם ; from H481 (אָלַם) (in the sense of tying);

H199.kjv_def: as for, but, howbeit, in very deed, surely, truly, wherefore.

H202.derivation: probably from the same as H205 (אָוֶן) (in the sense of effort, but successful);

H205.strongs_def: strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol
H205.kjv_def: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare H369 (אַיִן).

H5086.kjv_def: drive (away, to and fro), thrust down, shaken, tossed to and fro.

H5088.strongs_def: a promise (to God); also (concretely) a thing promised

H5089.derivation: from an unused root meaning to lament;

H5090.strongs_def: to drive forth (a person, an animal or chariot), i.e. lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed (i.e. impel or guide oneself); also (from the panting induced by effort), to sigh
H5090.kjv_def: acquaint, bring (away), carry away, drive (away), lead (away, forth), (be) guide, lead (away, forth).

H5091.strongs_def: to groan, i.e. bewail; hence (through the idea of crying aloud), to assemble (as if on proclamation)

H5094.derivation: (Aramaic) or נְהִירוּ; (Aramaic), from the same as H5105 (נְהָרָה);

H5095.strongs_def: properly, to run with asparkle, i.e. flow; hence (transitively), to conduct, and (by inference) to protect, sustain

H5096.derivation: or נַהֲלֹל; the same as H5097 (נַהֲלֹל);
H5096.strongs_def: Nahalal or Nahalol, a place in Palestine

H5101.strongs_def: to bray (as an ass), scream (from hunger)

H5102.strongs_def: to sparkle, i.e. (figuratively) be cheerful; hence (from the sheen of a running stream) to flow, i.e. (figuratively) assemble

H5103.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5102 (נָהַר);

H5104.strongs_def: a stream (including the sea; expectation the Nile, Euphrates, etc.); figuratively, prosperity

H5105.derivation: from H5102 (נָהַר) in its original sense;

H5106.strongs_def: to refuse, forbid, dissuade, or neutralize
H5106.kjv_def: break, disallow, discourage, make of none effect.

H5107.strongs_def: to germinate, i.e. (figuratively) to (causatively, make) flourish; also (of words), to utter

H5110.strongs_def: to nod, i.e. waver; figuratively, to wander, flee, disappear; also (from shaking the head in sympathy), to console, deplore, or (from tossing the head in scorn) taunt
H5110.kjv_def: bemoan, flee, get, mourn, make to move, take pity, remove, shake, skip for joy, be sorry, vagabond, way, wandering.

H5113.derivation: the same as H5112 (נוֹד)lemma נוֹדּ extra dagesh, corrected to נוֹד; vagrancy;

H5115.strongs_def: to rest (as at home); causatively (through the implied idea of beauty ), to celebrate (with praises)
H5115.kjv_def: keept at home, prepare an habitation.

H5116.strongs_def: (adjectively) at home; hence (by implication of satisfaction) lovely; also (noun) a home, of God (temple), men (residence), flocks (pasture), or wild animals (den)
H5116.kjv_def: comely, dwelling (place), fold, habitation, pleasant place, sheepcote, stable, tarried.

H5117.strongs_def: to rest, i.e. settle down; used in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, intransitive, transitive and causative (to dwell, stay, let fall, place, let alone, withdraw, give comfort, etc.)
H5117.kjv_def: cease, be confederate, lay, let down, (be) quiet, remain, (cause to, be at, give, have, make to) rest, set down. Compare H3241 (יָנִים).

H5122.derivation: (Aramaic) or נְוָלִי; (Aramaic), from an unused root probably meaning to be foul;

H5125.strongs_def: to resprout, i.e. propagate by shoots; figuratively, to be perpetual

H5126.derivation: or נוֹן; (1 Chronicles 7:27), from H5125 (נוּן); perpetuity,
H5126.strongs_def: Nun or Non, the father of Joshua

H5127.strongs_def: to flit, i.e. vanish away (subside, escape; causatively, chase, impel, deliver)
H5127.kjv_def: [idiom] abate, away, be displayed, (make to) flee (away, -ing), put to flight, [idiom] hide, lift up a standard.

H5128.strongs_def: to waver, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively (as subjoined)
H5128.kjv_def: continually, fugitive, [idiom] make, to (go) up and down, be gone away, (be) move(-able, -d), be promoted, reel, remove, scatter, set, shake, sift, stagger, to and fro, be vagabond, wag, (make) wander (up and down).

H5129.derivation: from H3259 (יָעַד) and H3050 (יָהּ); convened of Jah;
H5129.strongs_def: Noadjah, the name of an Israelite, and a false prophetess

H5130.strongs_def: to quiver (i.e. vibrate up and down, or rock to and fro); used in a great variety of applications (including sprinkling, beckoning, rubbing, bastinadoing, sawing, waving, etc.)
H5130.kjv_def: lift up, move, offer, perfume, send, shake, sift, strike, wave.

H5132.strongs_def: properly, to flash; hence, to blossom (from the brilliancy of color); also, to fly away (from the quickness of motion)

H5133.derivation: or נֹצָה; feminine active participle of H5327 (נָצָה) in the sense of flying;
H5133.strongs_def: a pinion (or wing feather); often (collectively) plumage

H5135.derivation: (Aramaic) from an unused root (corresponding to that of H5216 (נִיר)) meaning to shine;

H5137.strongs_def: to spirt, i.e. besprinkle (especially in expiation)

H5139.strongs_def: separate, i.e. consecrated (as prince, a Nazirite); hence (figuratively from the latter) an unpruned vine (like an unshorn Nazirite)
H5139.kjv_def: Nazarite (by a false alliteration with Nazareth), separate(-d), vine undressed.

H5140.strongs_def: to drip, or shed by trickling
H5140.kjv_def: distil, drop, flood, (cause to) flow(-ing), gush out, melt, pour (down), running water, stream.

H5141.derivation: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;

H5142.derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to the root of H5143 (נֵזֶק);

H5143.derivation: from an unused root meaning to injure;

H5144.strongs_def: to hold aloof, i.e. (intransitivey) abstain (from food and drink, from impurity, and even from divine worship (i.e. apostatize)); specifically, to set apart (to sacred purposes), i.e. devote

H5145.strongs_def: properly, something set apart, i.e. (abstractly) dedication (of a priet or Nazirite); hence (concretely) unshorn locks; also (by implication) a chaplet (especially of royalty)

H5146.strongs_def: Noach, the patriarch of the flood

H5148.strongs_def: to guide; by implication, to transport (into exile, or as colonists)
H5148.kjv_def: bestow, bring, govern, guide, lead (forth), put, straiten.

H5152.derivation: from the same as H5170 (נַחַר); snorer;
H5152.strongs_def: Nochor, the name of the grandfather and a brother of Abraham

H5153.derivation: apparently passive participle of H5172 (נָחַשׁ) (perhaps in the sense of ringing), i.e. bell-metal; or from the red color of the throat of a serpent (H5175 (נָחָשׁ), as denominative) when hissing);

H5157.strongs_def: to inherit (as a (figurative) mode of descent), or (generally) to occupy; causatively, to bequeath, or (generally) distribute, instate
H5157.kjv_def: divide, have (inheritance), take as a heritage, (cause to, give to, make to) inherit, (distribute for, divide (for, for an, by), give for, have, leave for, take (for)) inheritance, (have in, cause to, be made to) possess(-ion).

H5158.derivation: or (feminine) נַחְלָה; (Psalm 124:4), or נַחֲלָה; (Ezekiel 47:19; Ezekiel 48:28), from H5157 (נָחַל) in its original sense;
H5158.strongs_def: a stream, especially a winter torrent; (by implication) a (narrow) valley (in which a brook runs); also a shaft (of a mine)

H5159.strongs_def: properly, something inherited, i.e. (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
H5159.kjv_def: heritage, to inherit, inheritance, possession. Compare H5158 (נַחַל).

H5160.derivation: from H5158 (נַחַל) and H410 (אֵל); valley of God;
H5160.strongs_def: Nachaliel, a place in the Desert

H5161.derivation: apparently a patronymic from an unused name (apparently passive participle of H2492 (חָלַם)); dreamed;
H5161.strongs_def: a Nechelamite, or descendant of Nechlam

H5162.strongs_def: properly, to sigh, i.e. breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. (in a favorable sense) to pity, console or (reflexively) rue; or (unfavorably) to avenge (oneself)

H5166.derivation: from H5162 (נָחַם) and H3050 (יָהּ); consolation of Jah;
H5166.strongs_def: Nechemjah, the name of three Israelites

H5170.derivation: and (feminine) נַחֲרָה; from an unused root meaning to snort or snore;

H5171.derivation: or נַחְרַי; from the same as H5170 (נַחַר); snorer;

H5172.strongs_def: properly, to hiss, i.e. whisper a (magic) spell; generally, to prognosticate
H5172.kjv_def: [idiom] certainly, divine, enchanter, (use) [idiom] enchantment, learn by experience, [idiom] indeed, diligently observe.

H5176.strongs_def: Nachash, the name of two persons apparently non-Israelite

H5178.strongs_def: copper, hence, something made of that metal, i.e. coin, a fetter; figuratively, base (as compared with gold or silver)
H5178.kjv_def: brasen, brass, chain, copper, fetter (of brass), filthiness, steel.

H5180.strongs_def: something made of copper, i.e. the copper serpent of the Desert

H5181.strongs_def: to sink, i.e. descend; causatively, to press or lead down
H5181.kjv_def: be broken, (cause to) come down, enter, go down, press sore, settle, stick fast.

H5182.strongs_def: to descend; causatively, to bring away, deposit, depose
H5182.kjv_def: carry, come down, depose, lay up, place.

H5183.derivation: from H5182 (נְחַת); also (intransitively; perhaps from H5117 (נוּחַ))
H5183.strongs_def: a descent, i.e. imposition, unfavorable (punishment) or favorable (food); restfulness
H5183.kjv_def: lighting down, quiet(-ness), to rest, be set on.

H5184.strongs_def: Nachath, the name of an Edomite and of two Israelites

H5186.strongs_def: to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away (including moral deflection); used in a great variety of application
H5186.kjv_def: [phrase] afternoon, apply, bow (down, -ing), carry aside, decline, deliver, extend, go down, be gone, incline, intend, lay, let down, offer, outstretched, overthrown, pervert, pitch, prolong, put away, shew, spread (out), stretch (forth, out), take (aside), turn (aside, away), wrest, cause to yield.

H5188.strongs_def: a pendant for the ears (especially of pearls)

H5190.strongs_def: to lift; by implication, to impose

H5193.strongs_def: properly, to strike in, i.e. fix; specifically, to plant (literally or figuratively)

H5194.strongs_def: a plant; collectively, a plantation; abstractly, a planting

H5197.strongs_def: to ooze, i.e. distil gradually; by implication, to fall in drops; figuratively, to speak by inspiration

H5198.strongs_def: a drop; specifically, an aromatic gum (probably stacte)

H5200.strongs_def: a Netophathite, or inhabitant of Netophah

H5203.strongs_def: properly, to pound, i.e. smite; by implication (as if beating out, and thus expanding) to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon (inclusively, reject, let alone, permit, remit, etc.)
H5203.kjv_def: cast off, drawn, let fall, forsake, join (battle), leave (off), lie still, loose, spread (self) abroad, stretch out, suffer.

H5204.derivation: a doubtful word; apparently from H5091 (נָהָה);

H5207.strongs_def: properly, restful, i.e. pleasant; abstractly, delight

H5212.strongs_def: Nisan, the first month of the Jewish sacred year

H5214.derivation: a root probably identical with that of H5216 (נִיר), through the idea of the gleam of a fresh furrow;

H5215.strongs_def: properly, plowing, i.e. (concretely) freshly plowed land

H5216.derivation: or נִר; also נֵיר; or נֵר; or (feminine) נֵרָה; from a primitive root (see H5214 (נִיר); H5135 (נוּר)) properly, meaning to glisten;
H5216.strongs_def: a lamp (i.e. the burner) or light (literally or figuratively)

H5219.strongs_def: properly, a smiting, i.e. (concretely) an aromatic gum (perhaps styrax) (as powdered)

H5220.derivation: from an unused root meaning to propagate;

H5221.strongs_def: to strike (lightly or severely, literally or figuratively)
H5221.kjv_def: beat, cast forth, clap, give (wounds), [idiom] go forward, [idiom] indeed, kill, make (slaughter), murderer, punish, slaughter, slay(-er, -ing), smite(-r, -ing), strike, be stricken, (give) stripes, [idiom] surely, wound.

H5226.derivation: from an unused root meaning to be straightforward;
H5226.strongs_def: properly, the fore part; used adverbially, opposite

H5227.strongs_def: properly, the front part; used adverbially (especially with preposition), opposite, in front of, forward, in behalf of

H5228.strongs_def: straightforward, i.e. (figuratively), equitable, correct, or (abstractly), integrity

H5229.strongs_def: properly, straightforwardness, i.e. (figuratively) integrity, or (concretely) a truth

H5233.derivation: from an unused root meaning to accumulate;

H5234.strongs_def: properly, to scrutinize, i.e. look intently at; hence (with recognition implied), to acknowledge, be acquainted with, care for, respect, revere, or (with suspicion implied), to disregard, ignore, be strange toward, reject, resign, dissimulate (as if ignorant or disowning)
H5234.kjv_def: acknowledge, [idiom] could, deliver, discern, dissemble, estrange, feign self to be another, know, take knowledge (notice), perceive, regard, (have) respect, behave (make) self strange(-ly).

H5236.strongs_def: foreign, or (concretely) a foreigner, or (abstractly) heathendom

H5237.strongs_def: strange, in a variety of degrees and applications (foreign, non-relative, adulterous, different, wonderful)

H5241.strongs_def: Nemuel, the name of two Israelites

H5242.strongs_def: a Nemuelite, or descendant of Nemuel

H5244.strongs_def: an ant (probably from its almost bisected form)

H5246.derivation: from an unused root meaning properly, to filtrate, i.e. be limpid (comp H5247 (נִמְרָה) and H5249 (נִמְרִים)); and thus to spot or stain as if by dripping;

H5247.derivation: from the same as H5246 (נָמֵר); clear water;
H5247.strongs_def: Nimrah, a place East of the Jordan
H5247.kjv_def: Nimrah. See also H1039 (בֵּית נִמְרָה), H5249 (נִמְרִים).

H5248.derivation: or נִמְרֹד; probably of foreign origin;

H5249.derivation: plural of a masculine corresponding to H5247 (נִמְרָה); clear waters;
H5249.strongs_def: Nimrim, a place East of the Jordan

H5251.strongs_def: a flag; also a sail; by implication, a flagstaff; generally a signal; figuratively, a token

H5252.strongs_def: properly, an environment, i.e. circumstance or turn of affairs

H5253.kjv_def: departing away, remove, take (hold), turn away.

H5254.strongs_def: to test; by implication, to attempt

H5257.strongs_def: properly, something poured out, i.e. a libation; also a molten image; by implication, a prince (as anointed)

H5258.strongs_def: to pour out, especially a libation, or to cast (metal); by analogy, to anoint aking
H5258.kjv_def: cover, melt, offer, (cause to) pour (out), set (up).

H5259.derivation: a primitive root (probably identical with H5258 (נָסַךְ) through the idea of fusion);

H5262.strongs_def: a libation; also a cast idol

H5264.derivation: a primitive root; to gleam from afar, i.e. to be conspicuous as a signal; or rather perhaps a denominative from H5251 (נֵס) (and identical with H5263 (נָסַס), through the idea of a flag as fluttering in the wind);

H5265.strongs_def: properly, to pull up, especially the tent-pins, i.e. start on ajourney
H5265.kjv_def: cause to blow, bring, get, (make to) go (away, forth, forward, onward, out), (take) journey, march, remove, set aside (forward), [idiom] still, be on his (go their) way.

H5271.derivation: or נָעֻר; and (feminine) נְעֻרָה; properly, passive participle from H5288 (נַעַר) as denominative;
H5271.strongs_def: (only in plural collectively or emphatic form) youth, the state (juvenility) or the persons (young people)

H5272.derivation: from H5128 (נוּעַ) and H410 (אֵל); moved of God;

H5273.strongs_def: delightful (objective or subjective, literal or figurative)

H5274.derivation: a primitive root; also denominative from H5275 (נַעַל)
H5274.strongs_def: properly, to fasten up, i.e. with abar or cord; hence, to sandal, i.e. furnish with slippers
H5274.kjv_def: bolt, inclose, lock, shoe, shut up.

H5275.strongs_def: properly, a sandal tongue; by extension a sandal or slipper (sometimes as a symbol of occupancy, a refusal to marry, or of something valueless)

H5276.kjv_def: pass in beauty, be delight, be pleasant, be sweet.

H5278.strongs_def: agreeableness, i.e. delight, suitableness, splendor or grace

H5279.strongs_def: Naamah, the name of an antediluvian woman, of an Ammonitess, and of a place in Palestine

H5280.strongs_def: a Naamanite, or descendant of Naaman (collectively)

H5283.strongs_def: Naaman, the name of an Israelite and of a Damascene

H5284.derivation: patrial from a place corresponding in name (but not identical) with H5279 (נַעֲמָה);
H5284.strongs_def: a Naamathite, or inhabitant of Naamah

H5285.derivation: from an unused root meaning to prick;
H5285.strongs_def: probably a brier; by implication, a thicket of thorny bushes

H5287.derivation: a primitive root (probably identical with H5286 (נָעַר), through the idea of the rustling of mane, which usually accompanies the lion's roar);
H5287.kjv_def: shake (off, out, self), overthrow, toss up and down.

H5288.strongs_def: (concretely) a boy (as active), from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also (by interch. of sex), a girl (of similar latitude in age)
H5288.kjv_def: babe, boy, child, damsel (from the margin), lad, servant, young (man).

H5289.derivation: from H5287 (נָעַר) in its derivative sense of tossing about;

H5292.strongs_def: Naarah, the name of an Israelitess, and of a place in Palestine

H5294.derivation: from H5288 (נַעַר) and H3050 (יָהּ); servant of Jah;
H5294.strongs_def: Nearjah, the name of two Israelites

H5296.strongs_def: something shaken out, i.e. tow (as the refuse of flax)

H5297.strongs_def: Noph, the capital of Upper Egypt

H5298.derivation: from an unused root probably meaning to spring forth; a sprout;
H5298.strongs_def: Nepheg, the name of two Israelites

H5299.derivation: from H5130 (נוּף) in the sense of lifting;

H5301.strongs_def: to puff, in various applications (literally, to inflate, blow hard, scatter, kindle, expire; figuratively, to disesteem)
H5301.kjv_def: blow, breath, give up, cause to lose (life), seething, snuff.

H5303.strongs_def: properly, a feller, i.e. a bully or tyrant

H5304.derivation: plural from an unused root meaning to scatter; expansions;

H5305.strongs_def: Naphish, a son of Ishmael, and his posterity

H5306.derivation: from an unused root meaning to glisten;
H5306.strongs_def: shining; a gem, probably the garnet

H5307.strongs_def: to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)
H5307.kjv_def: be accepted, cast (down, self, (lots), out), cease, die, divide (by lot), (let) fail, (cause to, let, make, ready to) fall (away, down, -en, -ing), fell(-ing), fugitive, have (inheritance), inferior, be judged (by mistake for H6419 (פָּלַל)), lay (along), (cause to) lie down, light (down), be ([idiom] hast) lost, lying, overthrow, overwhelm, perish, present(-ed, -ing), (make to) rot, slay, smite out, [idiom] surely, throw down.

H5308.strongs_def: {to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)}

H5310.strongs_def: to dash to pieces, or scatter
H5310.kjv_def: be beaten in sunder, break (in pieces), broken, dash (in pieces), cause to be discharged, dispersed, be overspread, scatter.

H5312.strongs_def: to issue; causatively, to bring out

H5314.strongs_def: to breathe; passively, to be breathed upon , i.e. (figuratively) refreshed (as if by a current of air)

H5315.strongs_def: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)
H5315.kjv_def: any, appetite, beast, body, breath, creature, [idiom] dead(-ly), desire, [idiom] (dis-) contented, [idiom] fish, ghost, [phrase] greedy, he, heart(-y), (hath, [idiom] jeopardy of) life ([idiom] in jeopardy), lust, man, me, mind, mortally, one, own, person, pleasure, (her-, him-, my-, thy-) self, them (your) -selves, [phrase] slay, soul, [phrase] tablet, they, thing, ([idiom] she) will, [idiom] would have it.

H5317.derivation: from H5130 (נוּף) in the sense of shaking to pieces;
H5317.strongs_def: a dripping i.e. of honey (from the comb)

H5318.derivation: from H6605 (פָּתַח); opened, i.e. a spring;

H5319.strongs_def: properly, wrestled; but used (in the plural) transitively, a struggle

H5321.strongs_def: Naphtali, a son of Jacob, with the tribe descended from him, and its territory

H5322.strongs_def: a flower (from its brilliancy); also a hawk (from it flashing speed)

H5324.strongs_def: to station, in various applications (literally or figuratively)
H5324.kjv_def: appointed, deputy, erect, establish, [idiom] Huzzah (by mistake for a proper name), lay, officer, pillar, present, rear up, set (over, up), settle, sharpen, establish, (make to) stand(-ing, still, up, upright), best state.

H5326.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5324 (נָצַב);

H5327.strongs_def: properly, to go forth, i.e. (by implication) to be expelled, and (consequently) desolate; causatively, to lay waste; also (specifically), to quarrel

H5329.derivation: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)
H5329.strongs_def: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
H5329.kjv_def: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.

H5331.strongs_def: properly, a goal, i.e. the bright object at adistance travelled towards; hence (figuratively), splendor, or (subjectively) truthfulness, or (objectively) confidence; but usually (adverbially), continually (i.e. to the most distant point of view)
H5331.kjv_def: alway(-s), constantly, end, ([phrase] n-) ever(more), perpetual, strength, victory.

H5332.derivation: probably identical with H5331 (נֶצַח), through the idea of brilliancy of color;
H5332.strongs_def: juice of the grape (as blood red)

H5333.strongs_def: something stationary, i.e. a prefect, a military post, a statue

H5336.strongs_def: properly, conservative; but used passively, delivered

H5337.strongs_def: to snatch away, whether in a good or a bad sense
H5337.kjv_def: [idiom] at all, defend, deliver (self), escape, [idiom] without fail, part, pluck, preserve, recover, rescue, rid, save, spoil, strip, [idiom] surely, take (out).

H5341.strongs_def: to guard, in a good sense (to protect, maintain, obey, etc.) or a bad one (to conceal, etc.)
H5341.kjv_def: besieged, hidden thing, keep(-er, -ing), monument, observe, preserve(-r), subtil, watcher(-man).

H5342.derivation: from H5341 (נָצַר) in the sense of greenness as a striking color;

H5343.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5352 (נָקָה);

H5344.strongs_def: to puncture, literally (to perforate, with more or less violence) or figuratively (to specify, designate, libel)
H5344.kjv_def: appoint, blaspheme, bore, curse, express, with holes, name, pierce, strike through.

H5348.derivation: from an unused root meaning to mark (by puncturing or branding);

H5349.derivation: active participle from the same as H5348 (נָקֹד);
H5349.strongs_def: a spotter (of sheep or cattle), i.e. the owner or tender (who thus marks them)
